behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module boundary: summary-write path must read persisted per-dimension findings and enforce a cross-record invariant for the same round. A unit test of one function can miss repo/service wiring bugs. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: it only mentions stored findings/round linkage, not real OS/network/subprocess, and not real model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary service + findings repository against a local test DB. Seed one review round with mixed findings including at least one FAIL. Attempt to store a PASS summary for that same round. Assert write is rejected and no PASS summary row/state transition is persisted.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: Real failure arises when gate logic and storage interaction diverge (trusting summary text instead of findings). This is multi-module wiring (gate + persistence + summary source), so integration is the minimum. System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified: behavior is deterministic data-source selection, with no real infra or LLM-quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Persist findings containing at least one FAIL, and separately persist a manual summary text claiming all PASS. Run stop-gate computation through real gate service with repository wiring. Assert verdict is FAIL/BLOCKED based on findings and that changing only summary text cannot flip verdict to PASS.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans input validation plus audit persistence/emit path; the failure appears when override control and warning-record sink are not correctly wired. Unit tests can miss missing audit writes. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: explicit flag + warning record are deterministic local state concerns, not OS/network or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke summary finalization twice through application service: (1) manual override text without override flag, expect rejection and no finalize; (2) same text with override flag, expect accepted path plus persisted warning/audit record containing actor, round, timestamp, and reason code.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the review loop cannot finalize PASS unless a fresh review-agent round is executed on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Minimum real boundary is orchestration state across modules (phase tracker, diff/version marker, round records, finalize gate). You can catch this without real LLM calls by asserting freshness linkage (updated diff must map to a new round id/hash). Higher levels considered: Workflow/Agentic, but behavior text disqualifies them because correctness target is existence/freshness of a round on updated diff, not quality or variability of model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Create a run state with pre-fix review round and unresolved must-fix findings, then mark Phase 4 fixes with a new diff hash. Attempt finalize without creating a new review round: assert PASS is blocked. Then insert a new review round tied to the new diff hash and re-run finalize: assert transition can proceed only after freshness condition is met.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings, the flow emits an escalation outcome instead of PASS.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is policy evaluation over persisted round counters + finding severities + final outcome emitter. This is cross-module state handling, best caught in integration. System is disqualified (no required real OS/network/subprocess). Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text because decision is based on unresolved MUST-FIX state after exhaustion, not non-deterministic model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run state at max allowed rounds with at least one unresolved MUST-FIX finding. Execute final decision step through orchestrator + repositories. Assert outcome is ESCALATION (or equivalent) and explicitly not PASS; verify emitted outcome record/event includes exhaustion and unresolved-must-fix reason codes.
